questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: $\left(\begin{array}{ccc}2 & 3 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \\ 3 & 4 & ?\end{array}\right)$
questions.solution:
Here the number series is as follows-
$\begin{array}{ll} 
& 2^2-3=1 \\
& 1^2-2=-1 \\
\therefore \quad & 3^2-4=5 .
\end{array}$
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: $\left(\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & 3 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 6 & 10 & ?\end{array}\right)$
questions.solution:
Here we get the lowermost term by adding the two uppermost terms and doubling it .
As, $(1+2) \times 2=6$
$(3+2) \times 2=10$,
Similarly.
$(3+4) \times 2=14$.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
169 64 81 30
625 ? 49 50
1296 576 100 70
questions.solution:
First row
$\begin{gathered}
\Rightarrow \quad \sqrt{169}+\sqrt{64}+\sqrt{81} \\
=13+8+9=30
\end{gathered}$

Second Row
$\begin{array}{ll}
\Rightarrow & 25+\sqrt{?}+\sqrt{49}=50 \\
\Rightarrow & \sqrt{?}=50-32=18 \\
\therefore & (?)=(18)^2=324
\end{array}$

Third Row
$\begin{gathered}
\Rightarrow \quad \sqrt{1296}+\sqrt{576}+\sqrt{100} \\
=36+24+10=70
\end{gathered}$
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.q:
Directions: Select the missing number from the given alternatives:
366915
88119?
120?618
questions.solution:
The numbers in the matrix follow the below mentioned logic:

36 ÷ 6 + 9 = 15

88 ÷ 11 + 9 = 17

120 ÷ ? + 6 = 18

? = 120 ÷ 12

= 10

Hence, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

questions.q:
Directions: Select the missing number from the given alternatives:
285246
3585?
22?7112
questions.solution:
The numbers in the matrix follow the below mentioned logic:

(28 – 5) × 2 = 46

(35 – 8) × 5 = 135

(22 – ?) × 7 = 112

(22 – ?) = 112 ÷ 7

= 6

Hence, option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Select the missing number from the given alternatives:
38546179
21?1224
190914?
questions.solution:
The numbers in the matrix follow the below mentioned logic:

The value at the middle row is the sum of digits of top and bottom rows.

For first column,

Value in the top row =38 , Value in the bottom row = 19

Sum of digits = 3 + 8 + 1 + 9 = 21, is the value in the middle row.

For second column,

Thus 5 + 4 + 0 + 9 = 18 is the required value.

For third column,

Thus, 6 + 1 + 1 + 4 = 12

For fourth column,

Thus, 7 + 9 + x = 24

24 - 16 = x

= 8 is the required value.

The combination where sum of digits comes to 8 should be the correct match.

Only option C and D are such where sum of the digits of the second number comes to 8, but in option C the first value doesn't match with 18.

In option D, the first value is 18 and the sum of digits of second value is 4 + 4 = 8.

Hence, option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.solution:

Solving this we get:

16 + 9 – 12 = 13

Hence, option C is correct.

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Select the missing number from the given alternatives:
444937
52?41
583553
questions.solution:
444937
527741
583553

The above table is following the rule:

44 – 37 = 7 × 7 = 49

58 – 53 = 5 × 7 = 35

Similarly,

52 – 41 = 11 × 7 = 77.

Hence, there must be 77 in place of a question mark.

Therefore, option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
